clusters.headline: U.N. nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran, but Iran says only after final deal
clusters.summary: The head of the U.N.'s atomic agency said Iran's nuclear enrichment sites would be inspected as part of the interim deal. Iran insists any such visit would only come after a final deal.

clusters.headline: Trump airs grievances with NATO chief over alliance's lack of Iran war support
clusters.summary: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday, where he shared his frustrations with the lack of support he's received from allies on the Iran war.

clusters.headline: South Africa celebrates reaching World Cup knockout stage for first time
clusters.summary: South Africa’s team has celebrated reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time after defeating South Korea.

clusters.headline: Police seal off key roads in Nairobi as Kenya braces for Gen Z protests
clusters.summary: Protesters demand justice for more than 80 people killed during the 2024 demonstrations and last year's anniversary protests.

clusters.headline: Iran warns against Hormuz crossings without authorisation
clusters.summary: An oil tanker navigates the strait, reportedly hugging the western coast, despite threats from Revolutionary Guards.

clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: Unicef and Gavi Launch Call to Manufacturers in Support of Accelerated Access to Bundibugyo Ebolavirus Vaccines
clusters.summary: [Unicef] New York/Geneva/Copenhagen -- In a move designed to accelerate access to a vaccine against Bundibugyo Ebolavirus disease, UNICEF and Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance today announced the launch of a Request for Expression of Interest (EOI) to gather information from vaccine developers and manufact

clusters.headline: Africa: 655 Million People Still Living Without Electricity Underscore Urgent Need to Deliver On Universal Energy Access Target
clusters.summary: [WHO] At a time when energy security and affordability have risen to the top of the development agenda, 655 million people globally still lack access to electricity, and two billion use polluting fuels and technologies for cooking putting their health and well-being at risk. Sub-Saharan Africa bears

clusters.headline: Zimbabwe: Outcry As Chiredzi Man Spends Second Night in Prison Over Viral Mnangagwa, Chivayo Criticism
clusters.summary: [New Zimbabwe] A Chiredzi man arrested on allegations of incitement to commit public violence following a viral video in which he criticised President Emmerson Mnangagwa and businessman Wicknell Chivayo spent a second night behind bars Wednesday after failing to secure bail.

clusters.headline: Amazon, Microsoft cloud computing services should fall under EU tech rules, EU regulators say
Kenya: Court Frees Ninth Suspect in Utumishi Academy Arson Case Ahead of Friday Plea Taking
clusters.summary: [Capital FM] A Naivasha court has ordered the release of a ninth suspect linked to the deadly Utumishi Girls Academy fire after investigators found no evidence connecting her to the tragedy, even as eight other students remain in custody awaiting plea-taking on murder charges.
